title: Research Codex CLI update monitoring and recommendation workflow
description: Research and prepare a future Speckit feature for Codex CLI update monitoring. The follow-up feature should compare the locally installed Codex CLI and enabled workflow features against the latest official Codex release notes and selected upstream issues, produce a machine-readable report plus a human recommendation summary, and optionally create/update a tracked follow-up when an upgrade or investigation is recommended. Seed docs: docs/research/codex-cli-update-monitoring-2026-03-09.md and docs/plans/codex-cli-update-monitoring-speckit-seed.md
close_reason: Research completed; created durable docs foundation and follow-up implementation issue molt-2

title: Implement Codex CLI update monitor from Speckit seed
description: Create the dedicated feature branch/worktree and run the Speckit workflow for Codex CLI update monitoring using docs/plans/codex-cli-update-monitoring-speckit-seed.md and docs/research/codex-cli-update-monitoring-2026-03-09.md as inputs. The implementation should stay script-first, emit a machine-readable report plus recommendation summary, and keep issue creation opt-in.

title: Cleanup canonical root tracker after bare bd sync leakage hardening

Canonical root /Users/rl/coding/moltinger/.beads/issues.jsonl was dirtied by an earlier bare bd sync from a local worktree session without BEADS_DB loaded. Next step: recover/verify any residual leaked records and clean the canonical root tracker in a separate gated branch/worktree without manual blind edits in main.

title: Fix rca-index.sh octal parsing for zero-padded RCA IDs
description: Problem: rca-index.sh update fails on existing RCA IDs with leading zeros like 008 and 009 because bash numeric comparison treats them as octal. Evidence: during RCA-012 landing on 2026-03-12 the command printed value too great for base errors for 009 and 008, and docs/rca/INDEX.md had to be updated manually. Expected: rca-index.sh update and rca-index.sh validate both exit 0 and rebuild docs/rca/INDEX.md without octal parsing warnings for zero-padded RCA IDs.
notes: Acceptance: update exits 0; validate exits 0; no octal parsing warnings remain for zero-padded RCA IDs.

Current limitation: gh workflow run returns 404 until workflow exists on default branch.

title: fix(telegram): replace allowed_users with dm_policy + allowlist
description: Telegram bot sends verification code to ALL users including owner (ID 262872984). Root cause: 'allowed_users' is not a recognized Moltis parameter - it is silently ignored by the TOML parser. Moltis docs (channels.html) show the correct format: dm_policy = allowlist + allowlist = [...]. Fix: rename allowed_users -> dm_policy + allowlist in config/moltis.toml:378-381. Verified by consilium of 19 experts.
close_reason: Fixed: config/moltis.toml updated - replaced allowed_users with dm_policy=allowlist + allowlist=[262872984]. Committed in 9062f00, pushed to main, CI/CD will deploy automatically.

Background: New monitor expects webhook mode and active probe user. Current prod checks show Telegram inbound_mode=polling, getWebhookInfo.url empty, and TELEGRAM_TEST_USER is unset.

Required:

- Set webhook URL and HTTPS

Acceptance Criteria:

title: BUG: Moltis server config mismatch - black screen
description:

- bind = '127.0.0.1' should be '0.0.0.0'
- port = 38415 should be 13131

This causes black screen - Traefik routes to 13131 but Moltis listens on 38415 localhost.

title: Implement SearXNG self-hosted web search as fallback
description:
## Context

Brave Search now requires credit card (not truly free).
Tavily provides 1,000 free requests/month via MCP server.

## Goal

Deploy SearXNG as self-hosted metasearch engine on ainetic.tech server for unlimited, completely free web search.

## Requirements

- Deploy SearXNG via Docker Compose on same server as Moltis
- Configure as MCP server or HTTP endpoint for Moltis
- Aggregate multiple search engines (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, etc.)
- No rate limits, no API keys, no credit card

## Technical Notes

- SearXNG GitHub: https://github.com/searxng/searxng
- Can be added to docker-compose.prod.yml alongside Moltis
- Expose on internal port, integrate via MCP or custom tool

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SearXNG container running on ainetic.tech
- [ ] Web search working through SearXNG
- [ ] Fallback from Tavily when rate limited
- [ ] Documentation updated
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory

title: Implement Fallback LLM with Gemini API Direct
description:
## Overview

Add Gemini API as fallback LLM provider for Moltis to ensure high availability when GLM-5 is unavailable.

## Consilium Decision (2026-03-01)

**Architecture**: Gemini API Direct (NOT Ollama Cloud)
**Consensus**: 17/17 experts unanimous
**Confidence**: High

## Key Findings

1. Ollama Cloud does NOT support Gemini models
2. Gemini API has OpenAI-compatible endpoint
3. allowed_models is a HARD BLOCK - must add Gemini to whitelist

## Implementation Tasks

- [ ] Add GEMINI_API_KEY to GitHub Secrets
- [ ] Update config/moltis.toml with Gemini provider
- [ ] Add gemini::gemini-2.0-flash-exp to allowed_models (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Update docker-compose.prod.yml with gemini_api_key secret
- [ ] Update preflight-check.sh to validate Gemini secret
- [ ] Create failover-test.sh for testing failover
- [ ] Document failover architecture

## Acceptance Criteria

- Moltis switches to Gemini when GLM-5 fails
- Pre-flight validates Gemini secret
- Failover test script works
- Docs updated

title: Digest: healthcheck
description:
## Molecule Execution Summary

**Molecule**: healthcheck
**Steps**: 6

**Completed**: 0/6

---

### Steps

1. **[open]** Fix Critical Issues
   Address CRITICAL priority issues immediately.

Use bug-fixer agent:
  subagent_type: bug-fixer
  prompt: "Fix CRITICAL issues from bug-hunting-report.md"

These block release.


2. **[open]** Verify All Fixes
   Run quality gates.

Commands:
  pnpm type-check (or tsc)
  pnpm build
  pnpm lint

All must pass.


3. **[open]** Document Results
   Update health check documentation.

Actions:
- Update bug-hunting-report.md status
- Create Beads issues for deferred items
- Close healthcheck wisp


4. **[open]** Fix High Priority Issues
   Address HIGH priority issues.

Use bug-fixer agent for each issue.
Commit after each fix with /push patch.


5. **[open]** Run Bug Hunter
   Run bug-hunter agent to detect issues.

Commands:
  /health-bugs (inline orchestration)

Or spawn agent:
  subagent_type: bug-hunter

Outputs:
  docs/reports/bugs/YYYY-MM/bug-hunting-report.m...

6. **[open]** Review Findings
   Review bug-hunting report.

Actions:
- Categorize by priority (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW)
- Filter false positives
- Identify quick wins
- Create Beads issues for complex items

title: fix(ci): Add concurrency control to prevent parallel deploys
description:
## Problem
No concurrency control in deploy.yml. Multiple simultaneous pushes can cause race conditions and broken deploys.

## Solution
Add concurrency group to .github/workflows/deploy.yml:
```yaml
concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] concurrency.group added to workflow
- [ ] Second deploy waits for first to complete
- [ ] No more race conditions

**Effort**: 30min
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: CI/CD Architect
close_reason: Fixed: Added concurrency.group to deploy.yml. Parallel deployments now queue instead of race.

title: fix(security): Remove privileged mode from moltis container
description:
## Problem
moltis container runs with privileged: true. Container escape = host compromise.

## Current (DANGEROUS):
```yaml
privileged: true
```

## Solution
Replace with fine-grained capabilities:
```yaml
cap_add:
  - SYS_ADMIN
security_opt:
  - seccomp:unconfined
```
OR use gVisor/Kata Containers for sandbox.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] privileged: true removed
- [ ] Container still functions correctly
- [ ] Sandbox operations work

**Effort**: 4h
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: Docker Engineer
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory

title: chore(security): Backup encryption key to secure vault
description:
## Problem
Encryption key at /etc/moltis/backup.key is stored in single location. If server lost, key lost = all backups useless.

## Solution
Copy encryption key to secure offsite location:
- Use password manager (1Password, Bitwarden)
- Or secure vault (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
- DO NOT store in git

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Encryption key copied to secure vault
- [ ] Document recovery procedure for key

**Effort**: 30min
**Priority**: P0 CRITICAL
**Expert**: Backup & DR Specialist
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory

title: ops: controlled Telegram webhook rollout after polling stabilization
notes:
Progress 2026-03-07: polling stabilization codified in git (commit c724368, branch codex/fix-bot). Webhook monitor/enforcement removed; telegram allowlist normalized to string IDs. Remaining scope: design and validate webhook endpoint contract before re-enabling webhook in prod.
Progress 2026-03-07: added controlled rollout tooling (scripts/telegram-webhook-rollout.sh + workflow telegram-webhook-rollout.yml + runbook docs/telegram-webhook-rollout.md), optional deploy secret propagation (TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL/TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET), and manifest registration. External probe snapshot on 2026-03-07 shows POST /telegram/webhook and /telegram-webhook return 303 -> /login, so webhook cutover remains blocked until non-redirect callback endpoint is confirmed.
Related work moved to new Speckit epic moltinger-xtx (On-demand Telegram E2E Harness). This relation is contextual only; not parent/child.

title: bug(beads): investigate and fix shared redirect tracker leakage across worktrees
description:
Context
- During forensic analysis of root main .beads/issues.jsonl, multiple parallel worktree issue families appeared mixed into one tracker state.
- live `bd worktree list` currently shows several worktrees as `redirect -> moltinger` instead of isolated/local Beads state.
- This makes it unclear which branch/worktree owns which issue state and can cause hidden tracker drift in root main.

Problem
- We need to identify the exact source of this redirect behavior and stop unintended cross-worktree tracker writes.
- Possible sources include Beads worktree redirect config, daemon behavior, local tracker bootstrap rules, merge-driver interactions, or project-specific topology automation.

Required
- Reproduce the redirect behavior on the current topology.
- Identify the exact mechanism that routes worktrees into the shared `moltinger` Beads context.
- Determine which behavior is intentional vs accidental.
- Define the expected ownership model for issue state per worktree/branch.
- Implement a fix so worktrees either stay isolated or redirect only when explicitly intended.
- Add guardrails or diagnostics so future silent leakage is detectable early.

Acceptance Criteria
- There is a written root-cause explanation for why `bd worktree list` shows `redirect -> moltinger` for affected worktrees.
- The fix makes worktree tracker ownership deterministic and understandable.
- Root main tracker no longer silently accumulates unrelated worktree issue-state.
- There is an explicit validation step or test proving the corrected behavior.
- Documentation explains the intended tracker model and how to diagnose drift.
close_reason: Fixed redirect leakage and recovered issue into owner worktree

title: fix(deploy): Replace sed -i with GitOps-compliant approach
description:
## Problem
scripts/deploy.sh rollback() uses 'sed -i' which violates GitOps principles. Causes configuration drift.

## Current Code (BAD):
```bash
sed -i "s|image:.*:.*|image:...$VERSION|"
```

## Solution
Use environment variables or full file sync:
- Replace sed with heredoc or envsubst
- OR sync entire file from git
- Ensure server state = git state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No sed -i in deploy scripts
- [ ] GitOps compliance verified
- [ ] Rollback still works

**Effort**: 2h
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: Unix Script Expert, GitOps Specialist
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory
close_reason: Fixed: Replaced sed -i with MOLTIS_VERSION env var in docker-compose files. Rollback now uses env var instead of file modification. GitOps compliant.

description:

Backup restore never tested. Unknown if recovery actually works.

## Solution
Add weekly verification cron:
```bash
# /etc/cron.d/moltis
0 3 * * 0 /opt/moltinger/scripts/backup-moltis-enhanced.sh verify $(ls -t /var/backups/moltis/weekly/*.enc | head -1)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cron job installed on server
- [ ] Verification runs weekly
- [ ] Alert on verification failure

**Effort**: 2h
**Priority**: P2 MEDIUM

feat(logging): Deploy Loki + Promtail for log aggregation
description:
## Problem
Logs only local (json-file driver). No centralized logging. Server loss = log history lost.

## Solution
Add Loki + Promtail to docker-compose.prod.yml:
- Loki server for log storage
- Promtail agent for log shipping
- Configure Grafana datasource for Loki
- Add log retention policy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loki container running
- [ ] Promtail shipping logs
- [ ] Logs visible in Grafana Explore
- [ ] 30-day retention configured

**Effort**: 4h
**Priority**: P2 MEDIUM
**Expert**: SRE
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory

title: feat(monitoring): Configure AlertManager receivers for Slack/Telegram
description:
## Problem
AlertManager has no external receivers configured. Critical alerts not delivered.

## Solution
Add Slack or Telegram receiver in config/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml:
- Configure slack_configs or webhook_configs
- Add SLACK_WEBHOOK to GitHub Secrets
- Test alert delivery

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AlertManager receiver configured
- [ ] SLACK_WEBHOOK secret added
- [ ] Test alert successfully delivered

**Effort**: 1h
**Priority**: P0 CRITICAL
**Expert**: SRE, Архитектор
notes: Отложено: требует Slack/Telegram webhook. Вернуться когда будет настроен канал уведомлений.

title: feat(ci): Add pre-deployment tests to CI/CD pipeline
description:
## Problem
No tests run before deploy. Broken code can reach production.

## Solution
Add test job before deploy in .github/workflows/deploy.yml:
- Add test-unit job (bats-core for scripts)
- Add test-integration job (Docker compose tests)
- Make deploy depend on tests passing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test-unit job added
- [ ] test-integration job added
- [ ] Deploy blocked on test failure
- [ ] Basic script tests written

**Effort**: 4h
**Priority**: P2 MEDIUM
**Expert**: CI/CD Architect, TDD Expert
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory
close_reason: Implemented: test job with shellcheck, yamllint, docker-compose config validation. Deploy now depends on tests passing.

BUG: Telegram bot not responding
description:
Telegram bot configured but not responding to messages.

Potential causes:
1. No 'enabled = true' in [channels.telegram] section
2. allowed_users commented out
3. Environment variables may not be loaded

Need to verify:
- TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var is set on server
- Bot token is valid (curl api.telegram.org/getMe)
- Moltis logs show Telegram initialization
close_reason: Fixed: Added enabled=true to [channels.telegram] section, uncommented allowed_users

title: Stabilize GitHub issue labeling for GitOps workflows
description: Current drift workflow is non-blocking but may create issues without labels when labels are missing or token scope is limited. Add deterministic label bootstrap or remove label dependency across GitOps workflows.

title: feat(security): Configure Traefik rate limiting middleware
description:
## Problem
No rate limiting on Moltis endpoints. Vulnerable to abuse/DDoS.

## Solution
Add rate limiting middleware in docker-compose.prod.yml labels:
```yaml
- "traefik.http.middlewares.moltis-ratelimit.ratelimit.average=100"
- "traefik.http.middlewares.moltis-ratelimit.ratelimit.burst=50"
- "traefik.http.routers.moltis.middlewares=moltis-ratelimit"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Rate limiting middleware configured
- [ ] 100 req/s average, 50 burst
- [ ] Test: rapid requests get 429

**Effort**: 2h
**Priority**: P2 MEDIUM
**Expert**: Архитектор
notes: Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory

feat(backup): Enable S3 offsite backup for disaster recovery
description:
## Problem
All backups are stored locally on ainetic.tech. If server fails, all backups lost.

## Solution
Enable S3 backup in config/backup/backup.conf:
- Set S3_ENABLED=true
- Configure S3_BUCKET, S3_ACCESS_KEY, S3_SECRET_KEY
- Test backup upload to S3

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] S3 backup enabled in config
- [ ] Test backup successfully uploaded to S3
- [ ] Verify backup can be restored from S3

**Effort**: 2h
**Priority**: P0 CRITICAL
**Expert**: Backup & DR Specialist
notes:
Отложено: требует настройки QNAP S3/SFTP. Локальные бэкапы работают.
Приоритет понижен: стратегический pivot проекта → AI Agent Factory

close_reason: Fixed: Added comprehensive SECURITY NOTICE to docker-compose.yml documenting privileged mode and docker socket requirements

title: Fix browser compatibility issues (Chrome OK, Yandex password loop, Arc no load)
description:
Moltis UI shows browser compatibility issues:
- Chrome: Works normally ✅
- Yandex: Requests password (infinite loop?) ❌
- Arc: Doesn't load at all ❌

Likely causes:
1. WebSocket incompatibility (Arc)
2. Cookie/Auth header issue (Yandex SameSite policy)
3. TLS self-signed certificate issues

See specs/001-browser-compatibility/ for diagnosis plan.
close_reason: Fixed browser compatibility after proxy-header rollout: Yandex, Arc, and Chrome confirmed working by user with screenshots on 2026-03-07.

title: feat(llm): Add fallback LLM provider (Groq/Anthropic)
description:
## Problem
Only GLM-5 provider configured. If GLM API unavailable, bot stops working.

## Solution
Add fallback provider chain in config/moltis.toml:
- Enable [failover] section
- Add Groq (fast, cheap) as primary fallback
- Add Anthropic Claude (reliable) as secondary fallback
- Add GROQ_API_KEY to GitHub Secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fallback chain configured in moltis.toml
- [ ] Groq provider enabled
- [ ] GROQ_API_KEY secret added
- [ ] Test fallback works when GLM unavailable

**Effort**: 2h
**Priority**: P0 CRITICAL
**Expert**: Архитектор, Prompt Engineer
notes: Отложено: требует API ключи (Groq/Anthropic). Вернуться когда GLM будет недоступен.

title: Feature: On-demand Telegram E2E Harness
description: Spec-driven feature for on-demand Telegram E2E harness (CLI + workflow, manual verdict). Spec: specs/004-telegram-e2e-harness/spec.md
notes:
Implementation progress (2026-03-07):\n- Added CLI harness scripts/telegram-e2e-on-demand.sh with synthetic mode + deferred real_user contract.\n- Added workflow_dispatch pipeline .github/workflows/telegram-e2e-on-demand.yml with artifact upload on all outcomes.\n- Added runbook docs/telegram-e2e-on-demand.md and secrets/env documentation updates.\n- Added report sample contract specs/004-telegram-e2e-harness/contracts/sample-result-synthetic.json.\n- Validated success/timeout/upstream/deferred flows via local mock endpoints and CLI runs.

- Synthetic success path validated against local mock server (exit 0, status=completed, observed_response non-empty).
- Timeout path validated (exit 3, status=timeout).
- Upstream/auth failure path validated (exit 4, status=upstream_failed).
- Deferred real_user contract validated (exit 2, status=deferred_real_user, missing prerequisites listed).
- Unit quality gate executed via ./tests/run_unit.sh (all tests passed).

title: feat(config): Configure system prompt (soul) for GLM-5
description:
## Problem
No system prompt configured. GLM-5 has no role context, responds generically.

## Solution
Add soul prompt to config/moltis.toml:
```toml
[identity]
vibe = "technical"
soul = """
Ты - Молтингер, AI-ассистент для разработки и DevOps.
Пользователь: Сергей, timezone Europe/Moscow.

## Роль
- Помощник в разработке и DevOps
- Эксперт в Docker, GitOps, автоматизации

## Поведение
- Язык: русский
- Отвечай кратко и по делу
- Используй [OK]/[WARN]/[ERR] статусы
"""
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] soul prompt configured
- [ ] vibe = "technical" set
- [ ] AI responds with project context

**Effort**: 1h
**Priority**: P1 HIGH
**Expert**: Prompt Engineer
close_reason: Configured: vibe=technical + soul prompt with project context. Deploy triggered automatically.
